News.title: Clinton’s Epstein deposition released in full as shaky and raspy-voiced ex-pres talks about friendship with trafficker
News.content:
BILL Clinton’s deposition about his ties to Jeffrey Epstein has officially been released.

The former president spoke with the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform on Friday, answering questions for several hours.





The Oversight Committee released an over four and a half hour long video of Clinton’s deposition on Monday, a few days after it took place.

During the deposition, Clinton denied any knowledge of crimes committed by Epstein, a convicted sex trafficker.

“As someone who grew up in a home with domestic abuse, not only would I not have flown on his plane if I had any inkling of what he was doing — I would have turned him in myself and led the call for justice for his crimes, not sweetheart deals,” he said in an opening statement.

Clinton had a noticeably raspy voice as he detailed his relationship with Epstein and his longtime associate Ghislaine Maxwell.

Former president takes questions

When answering questions about if he had contact with Chauntae Davies, a 22-year-old accuser, he appeared shaky as he said he was not aware if Davies or anyone on a 2002 humanitarian trip to Africa was underage.

The 2002 trip including plans to visit several African countries.

Photos first captured on the flight for the humanitarian trip showed Davies rubbing Clinton’s shoulders.

“Although the image looks bizarre, President Clinton was a perfect gentleman during the trip and I saw absolutely no foul play involving him,” Davies said, according to a 2020 report from The Daily Mail.



“You’ve seen the pictures, there was one time when I was sitting up, I got a neck rub and I think Chauntae did it, but I’m not sure,” he said when asked if he had physical contact with her or anyone else on the trip.

He later said he had a neck spasm and “So they offered to help. And I let them help.”

He said that he did not recall any other massages taking place.

Clinton says he did not witness any sexual abuse

Clinton was later asked if he had witnessed any sexual abuse of any kind during the trip, which he replied “No.”



Three hours into the deposition, Clinton denied seeing Epstein do anything that he was suspicious of.

He then laughed off a question about Ron Burkle’s comments of Epstein being creepy and leaving the trip on a commercial jet instead.

“I don’t know, some people thought I shouldn’t fly with him,” he laughed.

The former president was also asked about a painting found in one of Epstein’s residences that appeared to be of him wearing a dress worn by Monica Lewinsky, a White House intern whom Clinton had an affair with while in office.

While listening to the House’s question about the painting, Clinton’s hand was noticeably shaking as he took a sip from a mug.



Clinton said he never saw the painting in person, adding, “If I had known Jeffrey Epstein had this painting, it would’ve saved me this whole day.”

Clinton also discussed his relationship with Maxwell, saying he considered her “a friend.”

He said that he had no communication with Maxwell related to young women or girls.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Clinton also confirmed that Maxwell attended his daughter Chelsea’s wedding.

“Looking back, I had no idea. If I had known what she was doing, I would have done the right thing,” the former president said.

News.title: Inside Iran’s drone blitz of RAF base as terrifying attack on Brits in Cyprus landed with chilling accuracy
News.content:
TERRIFIED Brits had barely ten minutes to take cover from an Iranian drone blitz that hit 900 yards from family homes on a UK military base in Cyprus.

The drone hit a part of the airbase used by Top Secret US spy planes after evading British defences at RAF Akrotiri.





A source on the base said “we are preparing for war” after commanders ordered military families to flee and the MoD closed three s`chools.

A defence insider fumed “it is embarrassing how little warning we had”.

A source in Cyprus said: “It all happened so quickly. Sirens, warnings, the sound of explosions.



“Everyone’s wondering how they got so close before they triggered an early warning.”

It is thought up to four Shahed style drones were fired at the base from Lebanon late on Sunday night.

They may have evaded air defence radars by skimming over waves of the eastern Mediterranean, a defence source said.

Ukraine’s President Zelensky offered to help as Shahed style drones attack his country almost every night.



The drone that hit Akrotiri was only detected about 20 miles out.

Sirens on the base started wailing at 11.45 and RAF Typhoons scrambled to try and shoot them down.

Just 11 minutes later, at 11.45pm, residents alerts on their phones to take cover under “substantial, solid furniture”.

A source said: “I saw the message at the same times as the explosion. It all happened at the same time. It was hard to think the sirens were so loud.”

Three of the drones are thought to have missed and ditched into the sea.



But one smashed into are next to the 2.4km long runway used by America’s U-2 spy planes.

The spy planes known Dragon Ladies are part of a decades long secret espionage mission codenamed Olive Harvest.

Defence Secretary John Healey insisted the damage caued “minimal” and he said there were no casualties.

It’s unclear if the US aircraft were the targets of the strike.

The attack came almost exactly an hour after Kier Starmer announced u-turn to let US forces us British bases in order to attack Iran.



Healey said his “best assessment” was that the drones were already airborne when Starmer effectively entered the fight.

Healey said: “The strike on RAF Akrotiri last night is deeply concerning, an example of the dangerous and indiscriminate attacks by Iran and its proxies across the region.”

He added: “Our best assessment is that the drone was fired before the Prime Minister’s statement last night on the US use of UK bases.”

The strike on Akrotiri came as fresh clashes erupted Hezbollah, based in Beirut and southern Lebanon, and Isreal.

Hezbollah’s leader Naim Qasem vowed to avenge the death of Iran’s ayatollah. Israel vowed to kill Qasem too.



Typhoon jets scrambled again on Monday as a second wave of attack drones hurtled towards the base.

Cyprus said the drones were downed.

But defence sources said the RAF jets were not involved and did not engage with their air to air missiles.

Thousands of military personnel, contractors and their families are based at RAF Akrotiri.

Hundreds of panicked relatives were moved to local hotels yesterday and warned they could be flown back to Britain in days.



The Sunday night strikes came hours after Prime Minister Kier Starmer reassured Cypriot President Nikos Christodoulides that his island was not a target.

In a message on Sunday afternoon Christodoulidies said the PM has “confirmed clearly and unequivocally that Cyprus was not a target”.

The Olive Harvest hangers are just 900 yards from family homes on English sounding roads such as Worral Avenue and Horsely Drive.

RAF Akrotiri of the British Sovereign Base area which includes a major GCHQ spy hub and a separate garrison at Dekhelia on the east of the island.

The territory became British when Cyprus gained its independence in 1960.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

And it is used to support to UK missions across the Middle East, including the wars in Iraq and Afghanistan, surveillance flights over Gaza, the evacuation of Khartoum and bombing raids against Houthi rebels in Yemen.

It is governed by a military officers and subject to UK laws

News.title: Twist in Fergie killer aide case as it’s revealed lover is murderer who battered man with mallet… before going for curry
News.content:
IT was a deadly combination of social ambition and an out-of-control temper that led former royal dresser Jane Andrews to murder her boyfriend in cold blood 25 years ago.

Details of how she fatally ­battered and stabbed rich businessman Thomas Cressman as he slept in his bed were dramatised in new ITV mini-series The Lady, which ended tonight.



Actress Mia McKenna-Bruce captivated viewers as Andrews in the incredible rags-to-riches tale that saw the Grimsby fashion student become an aide to then-Duchess of York Sarah Ferguson in the Eighties — and the horror that followed.

But the killer’s story took an even darker twist when, while in prison over the slaying, she fell for fraudster lag Mark Ellson, bombarding him with love letters and referring to him as her husband.

For we can reveal that the ­violent conman himself is now serving a life sentence for ­committing a separate, but equally callous, murder.



The Sun has interviewed relatives of his victim, coin dealer Giuseppe “Joe” Miceli, who Ellson beat to death with a rubber mallet during a botched bid to rob the safe at his bungalow in Duston, Northants, in July 2013.

In the 24 hours that followed, the monster repeatedly returned to the property to make sure Giuseppe was ­definitely dead.

Incredibly, it seems that Ellson only met his 71-year-old victim when he tried to pawn a gold watch that Andrews had given him during their six-year “relationship”.

Giuseppe’s retired gardener brother, Dominico Miceli, 81, said of the killer couple: “I think they deserve each other. They are both evil.

‘Easy target’

“The murders they committed were so brutal and the circumstances were similar. Andrews murdered her wealthy boyfriend out of jealousy, and Ellson killed my brother because he wanted to steal from him. He thought my brother was an easy target, but he picked on the wrong man.



“Knowing my brother, he probably refused to open his safe and he ended up paying with his life.”

Giuseppe, who was in poor health and used a prosthetic leg after a 1960 motorcycle accident, was about to retire when he was murdered. If he had sold Andrews’ watch, it might well have been his last deal.

Dominico is speaking at the Northampton home he shared with his brother when they both emigrated from Sicily as young men — and where he lights a candle in his ­sibling’s ­memory every day.

He says: “I would check in on my brother every Saturday, but on the day he was killed I was busy loading plants into my van so I could sell them the next day. I went over on the Sunday to check he was OK.

“When I arrived, I saw his car was outside, but nobody answered when I knocked. I went home to get the spare key, and when I got back and opened the door, he was on the floor.



“I feel guilty because the police say it took a long time for him to die and if I had gone to his house as planned, he might still be with us.

“Ellson had a curry after murdering my brother and I don’t understand how a person can do that. In court, he was cool, calm and collected. He has never shown any remorse.”

Jane Andrews was equally unrepentant about killing motor industry entrepreneur Tom Cressman, 39, on September 18 2000, when they returned from a holiday to Italy and the French Riviera.

Having been together for two years, she had hoped he would propose on the romantic trip, during which they spent time with his family.

But when they got back to Tom’s flat in Fulham, West London, he told her he had no intention of marrying her, sparking a furious argument and pushing the already unstable Andrews over the edge.



She had lost her lucrative job as Fergie’s dresser in 1997 amid rumours she had had a fling with Tuscan ­aristocrat Count Gaddo della ­Gherardesca.

And she was at risk of falling out of high society, having to return to her working-class roots, which she escaped when she answered an anonymous 1988 job advert in the now defunct Lady magazine seeking a dresser for Fergie.

Tom Cressman was her last hope of clinging on to her vainglorious status.

When he shattered the fantasy, she snapped, battering him with a cricket bat as he slept then stabbing him to death.



Yet at the Old Bailey trial where she was handed a life sentence in 2001, she claimed he was an abusive partner. And she protested her innocence throughout her 14-year stretch for murder.

Undermining her claims was her infatuation with former Royal Navy petty officer Ellson, who had a panic alarm installed and was given round-the-clock protection when she absconded from East Sutton Park Prison, near Maidstone, Kent, in 2009.

She fled the open jail in a rage when Ellson failed to make a scheduled visit.

It was not until three days later, when a taxi driver tipped off the police about her location, that she was found in a hotel six miles away meeting her parents.

‘Becomes obsessive’

Ellson’s missed visit led to Andrews somehow discovering he had a partner and two children — facts he seems to have kept hidden during their monthly get-togethers.



She repeatedly referred to him as her husband and fiancé in cards posted from her prison cell.

One creepy note included a snap of celebrities David Walliams and James Cracknell, but the heads were switched with photos of her and of Ellson and the words: “What a sexy pair.”

Ellson first started corresponding with Andrews before his 2007 release from a jail term for fraud.

He cut off contact in 2011, leading to a furious answerphone message in which she accused him of “f***ing up” her life.

In 2012, he gave a newspaper interview and revealed she had gifted him a £100 watch, warning: “I think it’s important that people get to know the real Jane before she is released.



“She has latched on to me because I’ve shown an interest in her. She then takes it too far and becomes obsessive. She wrote long letters, she was intelligent and she was ­eloquent . . . I can see how erratic she can be. Others need to be aware.”

Just two years later, he was standing before a jury himself, sentenced to at least 24 years for murder. Northampton Crown Court heard his victim Giuseppe died from ­multiple head and facial injuries, but would have been alive for “several hours” after the attack.



Before he entered the older man’s house, Ellson, from Wellingborough, Northants, had conducted internet research on how to incapacitate a man with a hammer blow.

He returned to the scene five times before he was arrested at the ­Cotswold Hotel in Nottingham, where he had paid £105 in cash for a room.

Today, another family member who wishes to remain anonymous claims that Ellson was mired in gambling debts, having separated from his wife, and he asked for Giuseppe’s help ­selling a fake Rolex.

This is believed to be the same £100 timepiece Andrews had gifted to him years before she was finally released in 2019, having been recalled to prison over harassment allegations. The relative said: “He thought there might be value in the Rolex’s precious metals content, even though it was a fake.

“Joe sent the watch away to have it valued and Ellson went round later wanting the watch back, but it wasn’t physically there. Ellson wasn’t happy about that and went back to his girlfriend’s house to grab a rubber mallet.



“I went to court every day and it was horrific. I was in shock.

“Nobody deserves to die like that. I vividly remember on the last day when the jury gave their verdict.

“Ellson was in the dock with two guards and they were laughing and joking over something. I looked at them and thought, ‘Oh my God, what a total lack of respect’.”

News.title: Moment Hillary Clinton explodes in rage during Epstein deposition – 'I'm done!'
News.content:
Hillary Clinton erupted with fury while giving evidence during her deposition over Jeffrey Epstein.

Last week, Mrs Clinton said she had "no idea" about Epstein or Ghislaine Maxwell's criminal activities in a dramatic evidence session before the US Congress. Bill Clinton also gave evidence.

Mrs Clinton's evidence was thrown into chaos when a photo of her testifying at the closed-door session was leaked by a Republican member to a MAGA blogger.

Lauren Boebert admitted to having taken a picture of Mrs Clinton during the session, sending it to influencer Benny Johnson who then shared it online. The hearing was briefly halted when the picture emerged.

Footage from the deposition has now been released, and shows Mrs Clinton reacting furiously at news of the leaked photo. You can view the moment roughly one hour and 19 minutes into the video below.

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

"I am done with this. If you guys are doing that, I am done. You can hold me in contempt from now until the cows come home. This is just typical behaviour," Mrs Clinton says.

Boebert admits to sharing a photo from inside the room, but says it was before the hearing began. Mrs Clinton then bangs her fist on the table. "It doesn't matter. We are all abiding by the same rules," she says before adding "I am done" and standing up from her chair.

During the deposition, Mrs Clinton said US authorities need to "get to the bottom" of allegations about Donald Trump and referred to claims the US Department of Justice withheld documents relating to the President being involved in "heinous crimes".

Mrs Clinton said: "A committee run by elected officials with a commitment to transparency would ensure the full release of all the files. It would ensure that the lawful redactions of those files protected the victims and survivors, not powerful men and political allies.

"It would get to the bottom of reports that DoJ withheld FBI interviews in which a survivor accuses President Trump of heinous crimes."

She also called for Trump to be quizzed under oath. She said: "If this Committee is serious about learning the truth about Epstein's trafficking crimes, it would not rely on press gaggles to get answers from our current president on his involvement; it would ask him directly under oath about the tens of thousands of times he shows up in the Epstein files."

News.title: Bill Clinton finally breaks silence on Epstein pool photo as deposition videos released
News.content:
Bill Clinton finally breaks silence on Epstein pool photo as deposition videos released

Newly released deposition footage captures the former US President being quizzed about the photo by a congressman during a closed-doors Washington DC hearing last week

View 3 Images Bill Clinton swimming in a pool with Ghislaine Maxwell

Bill Clinton has broken his silence on the now infamous photo capturing him swimming in Jeffrey Epstein's pool as videos of his Congress deposition were released.

The former US President was called to Capitol Hill to testify in front of the House Oversight Committee last week alongside his wife Hillary to answer questions about his affiliation with the convicted paedophile sex trafficker. He was asked about the photo showing him in a pool and reclining in a hot tub around halfway through his appearance.

Until now, the contents of the closed-doors hearing have been largely under-wraps, but new details have emerged today as footage of the proceedings were released.

Donald Trump spotted with nasty peeling scabs on his neck sparking major health fears READ MORE :

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

View 3 Images Bill relaxing in a pool, in an image released in the Epstein Files (Image: DOJ)

An unidentified congressman could be heard asking about the photo, saying the general public was interested in the "context" and "details" behind the snap. In response, Clinton says: "I don't think I ever knew the photo was taken." He adds that the photo, he believes, was taken in Brunei at the end of a "long" Asia trip.

He added: "The Sultan of Brunei was a man I had gotten to know well in my eight years as president."Clinton continues, saying that his trip to the hotel was arranged by the Sultan, who he said wanted to help with the Clinton Global Initiative, an international development foundation founded in 2005.

He said the Sultan had told him: "I want you to stay at this hotel and I hope you will use the pool. So I did. And then I got out, and went to bed, exhausted."

View 3 Images Bill Clinton has blasted Republicans and defended his wife (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

Article continues below

He was then quizzed about another person in the photo whose identity was protected by Trump's Justice Department, which released more than 3.5 million documents in its possession related to Epstein. Being named in those files does not suggest wrongdoing - and Clinton has never been accused by any Epstein victims.

He twice tells the committee: "I don't know who that is." He later clarifies that the other people in his party were in a nearby pool adjacent to the hot tub. No children were present among the group, he added, and was only briefly present in the tub.

He said: "I sat in the hot tub for five minutes, or whatever it was, and I got up and went to bed." The former President was quizzed by the same congressman about sexual activity, and was asked whether any had taken place that night. He denies anything had taken place.

News.title: United Airlines plane bursts into flames as emergency LAX landing and evacuation ordered
News.content:
A United Airlines flight was forced to evacuate after a fire broke out mid-air.

The aircraft was flying from Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) to Newark, New Jersey, when air traffic control received reports of a fire in its left engine. The plane turned back and landed safely at LAX at around 11.20am local time. Smoke could be seen billowing from the engine around 40 minutes later as the aircraft was parked on the runway, ABC7 reports.

All 256 passengers and 12 crew members evacuated the jet using emergency slides and air stairs before being taken by bus to the terminal, United said in a statement. The airline added that it was working to ensure passengers reached their final destinations as soon as possible. One person suffered a cut to their finger, but no other injuries were reported.

Image: ABC) ABC)

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

The Federal Aviation Agency said in a statement: "United Airlines Flight 2127 returned safely to Los Angeles International Airport around 11:20 a.m. local time on Monday, March 2, due to a left engine issue. Passengers deplaned on a taxiway.

"The Boeing 787-9 Dreamliner was heading to Newark Liberty International Airport in New Jersey. The FAA will investigate. Please contact the airline and airport for additional information."

News.title: Vladimir Putin crony warns WW3 will make Hiroshima look like 'child's play'
News.content:
Vladimir Putin crony warns WW3 will make Hiroshima look like 'child's play'

Vladimir Putin crony Dmitry Medvedev said in a blistering statement that western leaders were 'hardcore idiots and clinical b******s' who would drag the world into a new conflict

View 4 Images Dmitry Medvedev has issued a major nuclear warning (Image: Auto Lada/east2west news)

Vladimir Putin's Russian predecessor has warned that the globe is hurtling towards a World War 3 that would make the nuclear bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki look like "child's play".

‌



Dmitry Medvedev, a longtime Putin crony whose partnership with the Russian despot has allowed him to stay in power since 2008, has hit out against Donald Trump and his Iranian assault. Now deputy chairman of Russia's security council, the official, who is well-known for his inflammatory statements, said the US President's "insane course of criminal regime change" would lead to all-out war.

‌



He added after suggesting Iran could outlast an American assault that Trump had made a "gross mistake" by launching the operation he claimed was mean to maintain "global dominance".

‌



Donald Trump spotted with nasty peeling scabs on his neck sparking major health fears READ MORE :

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

View 4 Images Medvedev has enabled Putin's presidency since 2008 (Image: Kremlin/east2west news)

Speaking to the TASS news agency, he said: "This is a war of the US and its allies for maintaining global dominance. The pigs don't want to part with the trough." When asked whether World War 3 was on the horizon, he added that one would "undoubtedly begin" if Trump continues on his current path.

‌



He said: "If Trump continues his insane course of criminal regime change, it will undoubtedly begin. And any event could be the trigger. Any event."

Medvedev went on to call western leaders "hardcore idiots and clinical b******s" before claiming the US was afraid of Russia and "knows the price of a nuclear conflict". He then went on to say his predicted nuclear conflict would make the nuclear leg of its predecessor look like "child's play in the sandbox".

View 4 Images Medvedev said a future world war would make Hiroshima and Nagasaki look like 'child's play' (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

‌



He said: "In the event of its occurrence, Hiroshima and Nagasaki will be child's play in the sandbox [sandpit]." The Kremlin has also issued its own statement on the conflict on similar, but more restrained lines.

Russian top brass said it would result in a "humanitarian, economic and possibly radiological catastrophe" in the region, and accused both the US and Israel of "plunging the Middle East into an abyss of uncontrolled escalation". The statement from the nation's foreign ministry added that responsibility for the potentially devastating consequences of the war would lie on the two allies.

The ministry spokesperson said: "Responsibility for the negative consequences of this manmade crisis, including an unpredictable chain reaction and spiraling violence, lies entirely with them."

Article continues below

View 4 Images Russia has said Trump's operation would result in a 'humanitarian, economic and possibly radiological catastrophe' (Image: Jim LoScalzo - Pool via CNP/Shutterstock)

Iran is a key ally to Russia in both an economic and military sense, with both nations sharing international quasi-pariah status due to western sanctions. The nation under the now killed Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was providing Putin with battlefield munitions, primarily Shahed drones, to aid its illegal and murderous invasion of Ukraine.

Stunningly, Putin in his first comments following the Ayatollah's death, claimed the killing was "a murder committed in cynical violation of all norms of human morality and international law".

News.title: Iran declares Strait of Hormuz CLOSED and will 'burn all ships' – as prices skyrocket
News.content:
Iran has declared one of the world's most important shipping routes is closed - and says it will burn all ships passing through.

Ebrahim Jabari, a senior adviser to Iran's Revolutionary Guards commander-in-chief, said the Strait of Hormuz is now shut, according to Iranian media.

"The strait (of Hormuz) is closed. If anyone tries to pass, the heroes of the Revolutionary Guards and the regular navy will set those ships ablaze," Jabari said. It follows several blasts reported along the key oil export route in recent days.

The Strait of Hormuz lies between Oman and Iran and roughly one fifth of the world's total oil consumption passes through it. More than 20million barrels passed through every day last year on average.

Image: SEPAH NEWS/AFP via Getty Images) SEPAH NEWS/AFP via Getty Images)

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

It comes as gas prices have already leapt at the fastest pace since the outbreak of war in Ukraine, after Qatar halted production of liquified natural gas following attacks by Iran.

Oil prices also soared and global financial markets reeled from the fallout of the conflict between Iran and US-Israeli forces. The global benchmark Brent crude briefly reached $82 (£61) a barrel on Monday, following attacks on at least three ships near the Strait of Hormuz over the weekend.

European whole gas prices soared by 52% on Monday, marking the sharpest rise since prices were pushed dramatically higher by the Russian invasion of Ukraine in March 2022. The surge came after Qatar’s state-backed energy company QatarEnergy said it “ceased production” because of attacks on its facilities.

Qatari ministers had said earlier on Monday that an Iranian drone had attacked one of the company’s production facilities. Qatar is a major producer of LNG, cooled gas which can be transported via ships, responsible for about a fifth of global supplies.

Image: 2026 Planet Labs PBC/AFP via Get) 2026 Planet Labs PBC/AFP via Get)

On Monday in London, the price of natural gas for delivery in April was up by about 43% to 115p per therm. In the UK, gas prices are a key driver for the cost of domestic energy bills, indicating that a sustained spike could affect households in the coming months.

Neil Wilson, Saxo UK investor strategist, said: “Qatar is a top three LNG exporter, controlling roughly a quarter of expected supply over the next decade. Looks like Iran’s tactic is to pressure Gulf states so they in turn pressure the US and Israel to back off. I am much more concerned about European natural gas prices than oil prices, in terms of seeing a repeat of the 2022 European energy crisis.”

Global financial markets faltered after intense strikes across the Middle East and attacks on ships drove fears of energy supply disruption. London’s FTSE 100 was weaker as trading was knocked by the growing conflict between Iran and US-Israeli forces. The blue chip share index shed 130 points, closing 1.2% lower at 10,780.11.

Other European indexes suffered bigger drops with France’s Cac 40 down about 2.2% and Germany’s Dax tumbling 2.4% on Monday. But it was a more tentative start to trading over on Wall Street with the S&P 500 relatively flat, and Dow Jones dipping by about 0.1% by the time European markets had closed.

News.title: Former mayor caught by her own teenage son 'having sex with his friend at party'
News.content:
Former mayor caught by her own teenage son 'having sex with his friend at party'

Misty Roberts, 43, stands accused of having sex with a teenage boy at a pool party in 2024 while she was mayor of DeRidder, a city in Louisiana

View 3 Images Misty Roberts is accused of rape (Image: Louisiana State Police)

A former mayor is accused of having sex with a teenage boy at a pool party - with her children claiming they witnessed the alleged offence.

Misty Roberts, 43, allegedly carried out the offence at a party in 2024 while serving as mayor of DeRidder, Louisiana. Her trial on a charge of third-degree rape began last week following numerous delays, according to local media. Roberts resigned from office in late July 2024, days before she was arrested and charged with third-degree rape and contributing to the delinquency of juveniles.

Last week, jurors were shown pictures of the party in question, including of children holding drinks by the pool. In interviews played to the court, Roberts' son told investigators he saw his mother having sex with his friend through the crack of a window, while her daughter told investigators she saw her mother and the teenager "on top of each other", KPLC reports.

View 3 Images Misty Roberts resigned as mayor in July 2024 (Image: KALB)

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

However, on Thursday, when both of Roberts' children took the stand, her son told the court he was not certain what he saw that night. The prosecution presented a text message in which the son appears to tell Roberts: "He is seventeen." The alleged victim of this case was identified as 16 years old at the time of the alleged offence, according to KPLC.

On Thursday, the defence and prosecution questioned two forensic interviewers who had spoken with children connected to the case. One interviewed three children, including the alleged victim, in July and August 2024. The second interviewed Roberts' children in March 2025 at the request of the district attorney’s office.

Roberts' nephew told the court that he used his phone’s camera to see what was happening in the room that night. He testified that he was unsure if he had hit "record", but said that if he had, the video was never sent to anyone and he has since deleted it from his Snapchat memories.

When the defence asked Roberts’ nephew why he cleared his Snapchat before handing the phone to investigators, he said that he did it because it contained photos of him and his friends drinking, and he was worried about getting in trouble. He said he did not intend to delete any evidence.

None of the three witnesses who testified on Thursday said they saw any "private parts" of Roberts or the alleged victim. One witness said the teenage boy was shirtless.

After the alleged incident, the mother of the alleged victim texted Roberts to make sure she was not pregnant. The court was shown a screenshot of the message in which Roberts replied that she was on birth control.

View 3 Images Misty Roberts' trial began last week (Image: Facebook)

The court was shown that Roberts sent a screenshot of her conversation with the boy’s mother to a group chat with her friends, who responded by telling her to take Plan B. A DoorDash driver testified that he delivered an emergency contraceptive to Roberts' house.

The defence suggested in court that a key part of the interview with Roberts' son was not transcribed. Defence attorney Adam Johnson claimed the interviewer told the boy: "Just say it once, and we can move on." He also said the transcription notes are unintelligible.

Roberts had appeared in court in early February to enter her plea of not guilty to two felony charges of indecent behaviour with a juvenile and carnal knowledge of a juvenile.

In her resignation letter in July 2024, Roberts said: "For nearly 15 years, my love and passion for DeRidder has been my foundation while serving as Mayor. I will forever be proud of what we have been able to accomplish - together. This role has rewarded me with many great relationships.

"I am humbled to have witnessed the hard work that took a community to come together and overcome through unprecedented times. However, I must adjust my focus and priorities. Please accept this letter as my formal resignation, effective today.

"To the residents of this city: Thank you for your trust, love and support in me to lead our city into our future of greatness. My love for DeRidder will never waiver." Roberts was in the middle of a second term as the city's mayor, to which she was re-elected in 2022 with sixty per cent of the vote.

Article continues below

DeRidder is a city in Louisiana with a population of just under 10,000 people.

News.title: Donald Trump spotted with nasty peeling scabs on his neck sparking major health fears
News.content:
Donald Trump spotted with nasty peeling scabs on his neck sparking major health fears

Donald Trump appeared with visible scabs on his neck and discoloured hands at a Medal of Honour ceremony, with the White House physician explaining it's a preventative skin treatment

View 3 Images Sore, red rashes were spotted on Donald Trump's neck

Scabs can now be added to the mounting list of worrying health indicators surrounding Donald Trump.

‌



The president was spotted on Monday with dark red discolouration spread across his neck during a Medal of Honour ceremony at the White House. Running more than 40 minutes behind schedule, he once again displayed discoloured hands whilst presenting awards to military service members.

‌



White House physician Sean Barbabella said: "President Trump is using a very common cream on the right side of his neck, which is a preventative skin treatment, prescribed by the White House Doctor. The President is using this treatment for one week, and the redness is expected to last for a few weeks."

‌

View 3 Images Trump has been using cream to treat a skin condition, The White House confirmed

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings

The 79-year-old also appeared to stumble and slur his words whilst attempting to honour the final recipient of the ceremony. "Finally, we honour one more soldier, a fallen warria of world... of wars," he could be heard saying as he read from a teleprompter, reports the Mirror US.

‌



Trump has previously fielded questions regarding his health following severe bruising on his hands and what seemed to be an effort to conceal it with makeup during a televised meeting with Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelensky in December. The White House repeatedly brushed aside concerns about the president's wellbeing.

Press secretary Karoline Leavitt previously stated Trump's hand bruising stems from "consistent" irritation caused by "frequent handshaking and the use of aspirin". Several months ago, the president received a diagnosis of chronic venous insufficiency.

"Look, you see the president every day," Leavitt previously told reporters. "He's moving, he's working. There have been no adjustments made to his lifestyle."

‌



On Monday, Trump suddenly departed the ceremony without fielding any questions from journalists. At points, he strayed from the subject, pausing to praise his contentious ballroom development that will transform the White House's layout.

View 3 Images Trump claimed the US was prepared to continue attacking Iran for months (Image: Getty Images)

"See that nice drape? When that comes down right now you see a very very deep hole, but in about a year and half you're gonna see a very very beautiful building," Trump said. "And there's your entrance to it right there. In fact, it looks so nice I think I'll leave it and save money on the doors. I picked those drapes. I always liked gold. I believe it will be the most beautiful ballroom anywhere in the world."

Article continues below

He also addressed US-Israeli military action against Iran that resulted in the death of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. The Trump administration on Monday indicated it anticipated the operation to continue for weeks, with War Secretary Pete Hegseth announcing at a press conference that "Operation Epic Fury" would conclude within weeks.

Trump seemed to contradict Hegseth, stating the US stands prepared to "do whatever".

"We have right from the beginning projected four to five weeks, but we have capability to go far longer than that," he stated. "We'll do whatever. Somebody said, 'the president will get bored.' I don't get bored. There's nothing boring about this."

News.title: Major demand on heated tobacco devices as MP calls for supermarket advert ban
News.content:
EXCLUSIVE: Major demand on heated tobacco devices as MP calls for supermarket advert ban

The Government is facing calls to close a loophole to stop heated tobacco products - which heat up tobacco instead of burning it like a cigarette - being advertised in supermarkets

View 2 Images In June last year, the Government wrote to Sainsbury’s and Morrisons to ask them to stop “advertising and promotion” of heated tobacco products (Image: PA)

Adverts for heated tobacco devices must be banned, the Government has been warned.

‌



Health Secretary Wes Streeting is facing calls to close a loophole to stop the products being advertised in supermarkets. Heated tobacco products heat up tobacco instead of burning it like a cigarette.

‌



Dr Beccy Cooper MP, a public health doctor and Labour member of the Health and Social Care Select Committee, called on the Government to make the ban of such adverts “explicit” in the the Tobacco and Vapes Bill. The legislation is in its final stages of parliamentary scrutiny. Further debate will be held tomorrow.

‌



Dr Cooper told The Mirror: "The Government has been clear that it is illegal to advertise any tobacco products in supermarkets and that includes heated tobacco devices.

“Despite this, some supermarkets have continued to display ads for these products, even though the Government has told them it is against the law. The Tobacco and Vapes Bill needs to make this absolutely explicit."

View 2 Images “Heated tobacco products should not be conflated with vapes", a peer said (Image: BristolLive)

‌



Lib Dem peer Baroness Northover last week told the House of Lords that the  of heated tobacco products is “unacceptable” and called for the inclusion of the devices in the bill “without ambiguity or exception”.

She said: “Heated tobacco products should not be conflated with vapes. Vapes can be and are recommended for smoking cessation, following evidence, including a Cochrane review, showing that they are a helpful tool for smokers. Heated tobacco products do not meet that standard and are not recommended by NICE…

“If we are indeed serious about creating a smoke-free generation, all tobacco products, including heated tobacco, must be included without ambiguity or exception.”

‌



In June last year, the Government wrote to Sainsbury’s and Morrisons to ask them to stop “advertising and promotion” of heated tobacco products.

Adverts Philip Morris International’s (PMI) IQoS heated tobacco device was on display at their stores. At the time, PMI said it had "complied with all applicable laws and regulations" since it launched iQos in 2016.

A Sainsbury's spokeswoman said: “We offer a range of age-restricted products under our strict Think25 policy, including this heated tobacco device which is advertised. This is an alternative to cigarettes and is fully compliant with current legislation. We recognise the deeply complex and emotive nature of this topic.

Article continues below

"We remain in close contact with the Government and industry partners and are planning our transition to ensure we also comply with planned incoming legislation."

A Department of Health and Social Care spokeswoman said: "All tobacco products are harmful to health and there is no safe level of tobacco consumption.

"It is the Department's view that the advertising of any tobacco product including heated tobacco is already illegal, and our landmark Tobacco and Vapes Bill will make this even more explicit to put us on track to a smoke-free UK.”
